summary.narrative: The Masterpiece, Tsim Sha Tsui is a 124.7 sqm (3-bedroom) luxury residential apartment at 18 Hanoi Rd, Kowloon — one of Hong Kong's most prestigious residential addresses, situated within the core TST cultural and luxury retail district. The unit is currently leased to a senior vice president of a Swiss private bank at HKD 33,000/month (USD 4,244/month), generating annualised rental income of approximately USD 50,900 after property management fees. The existing lease expires October 2026; a strategic decision is required on whether to re-let at prevailing market rents, hold for owner use, or evaluate a disposal in the context of Hong Kong residential market softness. No mortgage is outstanding — the unit is held unencumbered.
summary.keyMessage: USD 2.72M unencumbered luxury asset · Annual NOI $50,900 · 1.87% cap rate · Lease expires Oct 2026 — re-let/hold/sell decision required by Aug 2026

basicInfo.location: Unit 12A, Tower 1, The Masterpiece, 18 Hanoi Road, Tsim Sha Tsui, Kowloon, Hong Kong
basicInfo.submarket: Tsim Sha Tsui (TST) Core — Luxury Residential
basicInfo.currentStatus: Leased — Swiss Private Bank VP, expires Oct 2026
basicInfo.ownershipSummary: Zhang Family Trust (100%) — direct registered title under BVI holding company

actions.title: Lease Expiry Decision — Re-let, Hold, or Sell
actions.recommendation: Current tenant (Swiss bank VP) has expressed interest in renewal but is requesting a 5–8% rent reduction citing broader HK market softness. Options: (A) Renew at HKD 31,000–32,000/mo (small concession, maintain occupancy), (B) Re-let to new tenant at market (risk 2–3 month vacancy), (C) Hold vacant for family use, (D) Evaluate disposal — HKD 21.2M may represent a local peak given HK residential market headwinds. Recommend engaging Savills HK for a formal advisory before August 2026.
actions.decisionNeeded: Family decision on leasing strategy or disposal — engagement of Savills / JLL required

actions.title: Annual Property Management Fee Review
actions.recommendation: Current property manager (Knight Frank HK) charges 2.5% of gross rent. Industry norm for TST luxury is 2.0–2.5%. No immediate action — review upon lease renewal.

linkedDocuments.name: Tenancy Agreement — Swiss Bank VP, Unit 12A (Nov 2024–Oct 2026)
Property Title Deed — BVI Co. registered owner
Knight Frank HK Property Management Agreement

topRisks.title: Lease Expiry / Vacancy Risk
topRisks.impact: Post-lease vacancy could result in 2–3 months lost rental income ($8,500–$12,700 USD) and additional letting fees 1 month rent.

topRisks.title: HK Residential Market Decline
topRisks.impact: HK luxury residential market has declined ~14% from 2021 peak; further deterioration driven by emigration and reduced mainland buyer demand.

topRisks.title: BVI Holding Structure Costs
topRisks.impact: Annual BVI company secretarial, banking and compliance costs ~HKD 28,000/yr ($3,600); manageable but requires ongoing attention.

marketSnapshot.submarket: Tsim Sha Tsui Core — Luxury Residential (>2,000 sqft)
marketSnapshot.assetVsMarket: Subject property: HKD 265/sqm/mo — at mid-market for TST luxury comp set
